indicator: ASCENT Ethiopia Electrification Program Approval effect on Mobile Money Account Rate
notes: Electrification is an enabling (not direct) relationship: reliable power supports agent uptime, device charging, and network coverage, which indirectly supports account access over a longer lag (24mo, matching multi-year program rollout). Estimate: +3pp on ACC_MM_ACCOUNT over the long run, set low relative to product-launch effects (e.g. IMP_0007=5pp for a second MM provider) since electrification is an indirect enabler, not a financial product itself. No comparable-country study directly quantifies this pathway; treated as a conservative theoretical estimate.
duration_rationale: Electrification is a multi-year infrastructure program, matches its own 24mo lag

indicator: P2P Transaction Count Surpasses ATM effect on Payment Method Crossover
notes: CORRECTED GAP: EVT_0006 (P2P Surpasses ATM milestone) actually had zero impact_links, not EVT_0007/0008 as originally (incorrectly) flagged. This link is close to definitional - the milestone IS the crossover ratio passing 1.0 (see USG_CROSSOVER, REC_0020), so evidence_basis is empirical and confidence is high rather than estimated. impact_estimate intentionally 0: this link is definitional/structural (the milestone IS the crossover ratio passing 1.0, see USG_CROSSOVER/REC_0020) rather than an incremental causal effect, so no magnitude estimate applies in the same sense as a product launch.
duration_rationale: Definitional/structural link, not a ramping effect

indicator: NFIS-II Strategy Launch effect on Account Ownership
notes: CORRECTED GAP: EVT_0009 (NFIS-II Strategy Launch, Sep 2021) had zero impact_links. A national strategy is a policy umbrella, not a direct product change, so its effect on account ownership is indirect/enabling with a long lag (~3yr) rather than an immediate, measurable jump. Estimated/theoretical since no source directly attributes an ownership increase to the strategy launch itself, as opposed to the specific products it enabled (Telebirr, Fayda, etc., which are already linked separately). Estimate: +5pp on ACC_OWNERSHIP, deliberately smaller than product-specific launches (Telebirr=15pp, Fayda=10pp) since NFIS-II is a policy umbrella whose concrete effects are already captured through the specific products it enabled. No comparable-country study isolates a national-strategy-launch effect distinct from its constituent programs; treated as a conservative theoretical estimate to avoid double-counting with Telebirr/Fayda's own links.
duration_rationale: National strategy effects are diffuse and slow to attribute

indicator: CBE Core Banking System Glitch effect on Banking Sector Fraud Losses
notes: High-profile, widely reported incident at Ethiopia's dominant bank (60%+ of deposits, 38M account holders) plausibly dented public trust in digital banking reliability in the following months. 'decrease' means the event decreases TRUST-pillar standing (i.e., worsens the trust indicator), not that it decreases fraud losses. Estimate: -10% relative effect on TRUST-pillar standing (proxied via fraud-loss trend), short 1-month lag matching acute reputational impact of a widely-reported single incident. No formal study quantifies single-incident trust erosion in Ethiopia's banking sector; treated as an approximate, qualitative-to-numeric translation, confidence medium.
duration_rationale: Acute reputational effects from a single incident fade relatively quickly
